description:
Machine-readable registry of all REST API endpoints and WebSocket events for the RFS Station Manager

All API endpoints are protected with rate limiting (≈1,000 requests per hour per IP by default (≈84 per 5-minute window), configurable via RATE_LIMIT_API_MAX environment variable).

Multi-Station Support: All endpoints support optional X-Station-Id header or stationId query parameter for station-based data filtering. Defaults to 'default-station' if not provided.

Authentication: When REQUIRE_AUTH=true, many endpoints require JWT authentication via Authorization: Bearer <token> header. See individual endpoint documentation for authentication requirements.

X-Station-Id.description: Optional station identifier for multi-station filtering. If not provided, defaults to 'default-station' for backward compatibility.

Authorization.description: JWT bearer token for authentication. Required for protected endpoints when REQUIRE_AUTH=true. Format: 'Bearer <token>'

billing.description: Stripe billing — Checkout, Customer Portal, status, webhook and audit trail. Owner-only except the signature-verified webhook. Returns 503 when STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is not configured.

POST /api/billing/checkout.description: Create a Stripe Checkout session for the org's chosen plan and billing interval (14-day trial). Owner-only.

200.description: { checkoutUrl } — redirect the browser to complete payment

400.description: Invalid planCode/interval or no Stripe price configured for the combination

POST /api/billing/topup.description: Create a Stripe Checkout session (mode: payment) to purchase an AI session top-up pack. Credits aiBonusSessions on the org after webhook confirmation. Owner-only; only available when aiEnabled and STRIPE_PRICE_AI_TOPUP is configured.

200.description: { checkoutUrl, packSize } — redirect to complete payment

400.description: AI not enabled on this org

503.description: Top-up not configured (STRIPE_PRICE_AI_TOPUP or STRIPE_SECRET_KEY missing)

POST /api/billing/portal.description: Create a Stripe Customer Portal session so the owner can manage their subscription/payment method. Owner-only.

400.description: Org has no Stripe customer yet (subscribe first)

GET /api/billing/status.description: Current billing summary for the authenticated user's org.

GET /api/billing/events.description: Owner-only audit trail of Stripe webhook events processed for this org (newest first).

POST /api/billing/webhook.description: Stripe webhook receiver. Verifies the Stripe-Signature against STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, syncs org plan/status/entitlements (checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.updated/deleted, invoice.paid, invoice.payment_failed), and writes an idempotent audit row. Requires the raw request body (express.raw registered before express.json).

200.description: { received: true } — always returned after audit; { duplicate: true } when the event was already processed

ai.description: Server-side AI gateway. Provider credentials live server-side; AAR Studio and the SPA call these instead of Azure browser-direct. When org context is present the aiEnabled entitlement is required; the monthly allowance (aiIncludedSessions) is metered in sessions and enforced at the speech-token endpoint.
ai.authentication: JWT bearer (org context optional — no org context passes through, consistent with entitlement back-compat)

POST /api/ai/chat.description: Proxy a chat/completion request to the configured AI provider (Azure OpenAI; Anthropic adapter stubbed). Requires aiEnabled when org context is present.

POST /api/ai/report.description: Generate an AI report/summary via the gateway. Requires aiEnabled when org context is present.

POST /api/ai/speech/token.description: Vend a short-lived Azure Speech authorization token for browser SDK use. Counts as one metered AI session and enforces the monthly allowance.

200.description: { token, region } — short-lived Speech auth token

402.description: Monthly AI session allowance (included + bonus) exhausted — { remaining, included, bonus, resetAt, topupAvailable }

GET /api/ai/usage.description: AI sessions used this month and the remaining monthly allowance for the org (drives the /admin/organization meter).

200.description: { used, included, bonus, remaining, resetAt, aiEnabled } — bonus is purchased top-up sessions not yet consumed

aar-sessions.description: Server-side persistence for AAR Studio reviews, scoped to the owning Organization so a review survives device loss and is visible brigade-wide instead of living only in one facilitator's localStorage. AAR Studio remains local-first: it works fully signed-out against localStorage and mirrors to these endpoints best-effort when an SM JWT is present. The session body is stored as an opaque JSON blob (payload) with a few fields lifted out for listing.

GET /api/aar-sessions.description: List this org's saved reviews as summaries (no payload), newest-updated first.

403.description: No organization context, or aarStudioEnabled entitlement required

GET /api/aar-sessions/:id.description: Fetch one saved review including the full payload, scoped to the caller's org.

403.description: No organization context, or aarStudioEnabled entitlement required

404.description: Review not found in this org

PUT /api/aar-sessions/:id.description: Create or replace a review (the client owns the id). Org scoping is enforced; creation metadata (createdAt/createdBy) is preserved across updates. Optimistic concurrency: when clientUpdatedAt is supplied and an existing row was edited more recently, the save is rejected (409) instead of clobbering it; omit clientUpdatedAt to force last-write-wins.

requestBody.title: string
requestBody.payload: string (JSON of the AAR Studio session)
requestBody.clientUpdatedAt: string? (the saving device's last-edit time, ISO; enables stale-write detection)

403.description: No organization context, or aarStudioEnabled entitlement required

409.description: Stale write — updated more recently on another device; body is { error, current } (current omits payload)

413.description: Review payload too large to persist (~900k char cap)

DELETE /api/aar-sessions/:id.description: Delete a review, scoped to the caller's org.

403.description: No organization context, or aarStudioEnabled entitlement required

404.description: Review not found in this org

truck-check-vehicle-types.description: TC-1 vehicle types + resolved checklists. A VehicleType owns a locked standard checklist (immutable for adopting brigades, stable itemCodes for cross-brigade comparison); isStandard publishes it for other orgs to adopt. An appliance links to a type via vehicleTypeId and the effective checklist = the type's standard items + the brigade's custom overlay, in saved order.
truck-check-vehicle-types.authentication: JWT (vehicle-type writes require admin + org ownership; the effective-checklist endpoints are kiosk-friendly)

GET /api/truck-checks/vehicle-types.description: List the org's vehicle types plus all published standards.

POST /api/truck-checks/vehicle-types.description: Create a vehicle type owned by the caller's org (isStandard publishes it).

PUT /api/truck-checks/vehicle-types/:id.description: Update a vehicle type the org owns (re-normalises standard item codes).

DELETE /api/truck-checks/vehicle-types/:.description: Delete a vehicle type the org owns.

GET /api/truck-checks/appliances/:id/che.description: The resolved effective checklist for an appliance (locked standard items + brigade custom overlay, in saved order). What the check workflow renders.

PUT /api/truck-checks/appliances/:id/che.description: Save the brigade's custom overlay (custom items + preferred order). Standard items are never sent here; they live on the type.

requestBody.itemOrder: string[] (standard item itemCode or custom item id)

truck-check-appliance-zones.description: A1 per-truck spatial model. An ApplianceZone is a named physical area on exactly ONE appliance (Pump Panel, Driver-side Locker 1, Cab), with a walk-around order and an optional canonical zoneCode slug for cross-brigade analytics. Admin-authored config; reads require an authenticated session. See docs/archive/AI_MAINTENANCE_AGENT_DESIGN.md §4.1.
truck-check-appliance-zones.authentication: JWT (zone writes require admin; list requires an authenticated session)

GET /api/truck-checks/appliances/:applia.description: List one appliance's zones in walk-around order.

POST /api/truck-checks/appliances/:appli.description: Create a zone on an appliance (stationId inherited from the truck; order defaults to the end; zoneCode derived from the name when not given).

PUT /api/truck-checks/zones/:id.description: Update a zone (partial patch; blank zoneCode is treated as not-provided).

POST /api/truck-checks/appliances/:id/se.description: Seed an appliance's zones from the starter vocabulary for its VehicleType.code (zoneVocabulary.ts). Idempotent by default — returns existing zones unchanged. Pass ?replace=true to delete and re-seed. Returns { seeded: number, zones: ApplianceZone[], message?: string }. Also triggered automatically (non-fatal) on POST /appliances when vehicleTypeId is supplied.

queryParams.replace: boolean? — if true, deletes existing zones before seeding

200.description: { seeded: number, zones: ApplianceZone[], message?: string }

422.description: No vehicleTypeId set on appliance, or vehicle type record not found

truck-check-appliance-equipment.description: A1 per-truck inventory. An ApplianceEquipment is a piece of equipment on exactly ONE appliance (Holmatro spreader, BA set #2, Akron branch), optionally located in a zone (zoneId). active:false retires an item without losing history. Admin-authored config; reads require an authenticated session. See docs/archive/AI_MAINTENANCE_AGENT_DESIGN.md §4.2.
truck-check-appliance-equipment.authentication: JWT (equipment writes require admin; list requires an authenticated session)

GET /api/truck-checks/appliances/:applia.description: List one appliance's equipment (active only by default; ?includeInactive=true to include retired).

POST /api/truck-checks/appliances/:appli.description: Add equipment to an appliance (stationId inherited from the truck; equipmentCode derived from the name when not given; active defaults true).

PUT /api/truck-checks/equipment/:id.description: Update an equipment item (partial patch; set active:false to retire while keeping history).

DELETE /api/truck-checks/equipment/:id.description: Delete an equipment item (prefer active:false to retain history).

truck-check-issues.description: TC-4 issue follow-up. A CheckResult with status 'issue' starts an 'open' lifecycle; an equipment officer moves it open → acknowledged → resolved (admin-gated via the parent mount; no separate role). Member attribution (TC-3): a check run's completedBy holds the roster memberId when the inspector is picked from the roster, else the free-text name (kiosk).

GET /api/truck-checks/issues.description: Station-scoped flat feed of issue results enriched with appliance/run context for the equipment-officer follow-up view. ?status=open|acknowledged|resolved; defaults to outstanding (open+acknowledged).

PATCH /api/truck-checks/results/:id/issu.description: Update an issue result's lifecycle (issueStatus/issueNote/assignedTo/resolvedBy). Resolving stamps resolvedAt. Body must include runId (Table Storage locates the result by run partition).

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/members.description: Get all registered members sorted by activity level

POST /api/members/:id/invite.description: Generate a one-time activation invite URL for a member (admin-only). Sets authStatus='invited' and inviteToken on the member. The returned inviteUrl points to /activate/:token. Token is consumed on first successful activation.

email.description: Optional email to record on the member for reference

GET /api/members/activate/:token.description: Public endpoint. Validate an invite token and return member name and org name for the activation form. No authentication required.

404.description: Token not found or already used

POST /api/members/activate/:token.description: Public endpoint. Complete member activation: creates an AdminUser with role=viewer, marks Member.authStatus=active, clears inviteToken. No authentication required.

404.description: Token not found or already used

DELETE /api/members/:id.description: Soft delete a member (mark inactive, hide from UI, retain history)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)
Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

POST /api/members/import.description: Validate CSV file for bulk member import

file.description: CSV file with columns: First Name, Last Name, Rank, Roles

400.description: No file uploaded or CSV parsing failed

POST /api/members/import/execute.description: Execute bulk member import after validation

Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

DELETE /api/activities/:activityId.description: Soft delete an activity (hidden from lists, retained for history). Station-scoped: only activities visible to the requesting station can be deleted.

404.description: Activity not found (or belongs to another station)

GET /api/checkins.description: Get all check-ins (both active and inactive)

Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

POST /api/checkins.description: Check in member or undo existing check-in (toggle)

activityId.description: Activity UUID (uses active if not provided)

400.description: Invalid request or no active activity

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)
Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

POST /api/checkins/url-checkin.description: Check in via sign-in link/QR using member ID (preferred) with legacy name fallback; station-specific

identifier.description: Member ID from sign-in link/QR (preferred); legacy member name accepted for backward compatibility

stationId.description: Station ID for brigade-specific check-in; overrides X-Station-Id header when provided

400.description: Invalid identifier or no active activity

404.description: Member not found for resolved station

PUT /api/events/:id/end.description: End an active event (marks as inactive, event remains visible)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

PUT /api/events/:id/reactivate.description: Reactivate an ended event (clears end time, sets isActive=true)
PUT /api/events/:id/reactivate.notes: Events are automatically deactivated after EVENT_EXPIRY_HOURS (default: 12). Use this endpoint to reactivate expired events.

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

POST /api/events/admin/rollover.description: Manually trigger event expiry check (marks expired events as inactive)
POST /api/events/admin/rollover.notes: Events older than EVENT_EXPIRY_HOURS (default: 12) are automatically marked as inactive. Expired events remain visible in the UI.

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

POST /api/events/:id/participants.description: Add participant to event (check-in). If participant already exists, toggles and removes them (undo check-in). Creates audit log entry for traceability.

performedBy.description: User ID or name of person performing the action (optional)

notes.description: Optional notes or reason for action (optional)

action.description: Action performed (added or removed if toggled)

400.description: Invalid request or event not active

auditLog.description: Creates EventAuditLog entry for both additions and removals (toggle undo)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

DELETE /api/events/:id/participants/:par.description: Remove participant from event (check out). Creates audit log entry for traceability.

performedBy.description: User ID or name of person performing the removal (optional)

notes.description: Optional reason or notes for the removal (optional)

auditLog.description: Creates EventAuditLog entry for compliance and traceability

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/events/:eventId/audit.description: Get audit log of all participant additions and removals for an event. Returns chronological sequence of membership changes.

totalLogs.description: Total number of audit log entries

logs.description: Array of audit log entries in chronological order

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)
Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/reports/attendance-summary.description: Get monthly attendance statistics grouped by month

startDate.description: Start date for report range (ISO 8601). Defaults to 30 days ago.

endDate.description: End date for report range (ISO 8601). Defaults to now.

GET /api/reports/member-participation.description: Get top members by participation count

limit.description: Maximum number of members to return

GET /api/reports/activity-breakdown.description: Get breakdown of activities by category

GET /api/stations/lookup.description: Search and locate fire service stations nationally with geolocation sorting. Supports all Australian states and territories.

400.description: Invalid parameters (missing both query and location)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

notes: Returns merged results: closest stations by distance + matching search results
Results are deduplicated by station ID
Distance calculated using Haversine formula
Search is case-insensitive and matches name, suburb, or brigade
Performance target: < 200ms response time

GET /api/stations/count.description: Get total count of loaded fire service stations (debugging/monitoring)

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

GET /api/stations/demo.description: Get the demo station for public viewing (unauthenticated access). Returns only the demo station with sample data for demonstration purposes.

rateLimiting.description: Rate limited to ≈1,000 requests per hour per IP (≈84 per 5-minute window) (configurable)

notes: Always accessible without authentication
Returns demo-station ID with sample/fake data
Used for public landing page when REQUIRE_AUTH=true

GET /api/stations.description: Get all stations with optional filtering and pagination

notes: Requires authentication when REQUIRE_AUTH=true environment variable is set

GET /api/stations/brigade/:brigadeId.description: Get all stations in a brigade

200.description: Array of stations in the brigade

notes: Requires authentication when REQUIRE_AUTH=true environment variable is set

GET /api/stations/check-brigade/:brigade.description: Check if a station with this brigade ID already exists. Used for duplicate prevention during station creation.

200.description: Station exists with this brigade ID

message.example: Station "Horsley Park Rural Fire Brigade" already exists with this brigade ID

404.description: No active station found with this brigade ID

message.example: No active station found with this brigade ID

notes: Requires authentication when REQUIRE_AUTH=true environment variable is set

POST /api/stations.description: Create a new station. Validates that brigade ID is unique to prevent duplicate station creation. Returns 409 if a station with the same brigade ID already exists.

409.description: Station already exists with this brigade ID

message.example: A station with brigade ID "horsley-park-rural-fire-brigade" already exists: Horsley Park Rural Fire Brigade

DELETE /api/stations/:id.description: Soft delete a station (sets isActive=false). Prevents deletion if station has members, active check-ins, or events.

400.description: Cannot delete station with existing data

POST /api/stations/demo/reset.description: Reset demo station with fresh sample data. Deletes all demo station data and recreates it with realistic samples including members, activities, events, and appliances.
notes: Demo station ID is 'demo-station'
Creates 20 diverse sample members with various ranks
Creates 5 standard activities
Creates 10 recent check-ins
Creates 3-4 events (1 active, rest completed)
Creates 2 appliances with sample check runs
Operation may take several seconds to complete
Emits 'demo-station-reset' WebSocket event on success

POST /api/brigade-access/generate.description: Generate a new brigade access token for kiosk mode. Tokens lock the sign-in interface to a specific station.

brigadeId.description: Brigade ID to associate with the token

stationId.description: Station ID to lock access to

description.description: Optional description for the token (e.g., 'Main Kiosk')

expiresInDays.description: Optional expiration period in days (default: no expiration)

kioskUrl.description: Full kiosk sign-in URL with token

notes: No authentication required per current design
Should be protected with admin authentication in production
Tokens are UUIDs (128-bit random) for security

POST /api/brigade-access/validate.description: Validate a brigade access token and return associated station info

notes: Public endpoint used by kiosk mode middleware
Expired tokens are automatically removed
Should be protected with admin authentication in production

GET /api/brigade-access/brigade/:brigade.description: Get all active tokens for a brigade

notes: Returns only non-expired tokens
Should be protected with admin authentication in production

GET /api/brigade-access/station/:station.description: Get all active tokens for a station

notes: Returns only non-expired tokens
Should be protected with admin authentication in production
Public endpoint for monitoring
Automatically cleans up expired tokens before counting

GET /api/brigade-access/all-tokens.description: Get all active tokens across all stations (admin utility). Returns tokens with full kiosk URLs for easy distribution.

kioskUrl.description: Full kiosk sign-in URL with embedded token

notes: Master admin utility endpoint
Returns only non-expired tokens
Should be protected with admin authentication in production
Used by /admin/brigade-access frontend page
Automatically cleans up expired tokens before returning results
Each token includes pre-formatted kiosk URL for easy copy/paste
Frontend provides QR code generation and copy-to-clipboard features

demo-station-reset.description: Demo station has been reset with fresh data

BillingEvent.description: Audit row for a received Stripe webhook event (including failures), used for idempotency and troubleshooting.

organizationId.description: Org the event applies to; absent when the event carries no org metadata

AARSession.description: A persisted AAR Studio review, scoped to the owning organization. The full client-side session is stored verbatim as a JSON string in payload; the other fields are denormalised for listing and access control. In Table Storage the payload is chunked across payload0..payloadN properties to stay under the per-property/entity size limits; this is an implementation detail not exposed by the API.

id.description: Client-owned review id (AAR Studio generates it)

stationId.description: Optional brigade/station the review belongs to

payload.description: JSON of the full AAR Studio session object

createdBy.description: userId of the facilitator who first saved it

clientUpdatedAt.description: The review's last-edit time on the saving device; used for optimistic-concurrency conflict detection (distinct from updatedAt, the server write time)

ChecklistItem.description: One check on a checklist. itemCode is a stable slug enabling cross-brigade aggregation; isStandard marks an item that comes from a VehicleType's locked standard checklist.

VehicleType.description: A standard vehicle type owning a locked standard checklist. Brigades adopt a type for their vehicles and add/reorder custom items but never edit the standard items (the locked core that enables cross-brigade comparison). isStandard publishes it for other orgs to adopt.

organizationId.description: Owning org; absent for a global/standard type

standardItems.description: Locked standard checks, each with a stable itemCode and isStandard:true

EffectiveChecklist.description: The resolved checklist for an appliance: the vehicle type's locked standard items merged with the brigade's custom overlay, in saved order. Computed, not stored.

items.description: Each carries isStandard so the UI can lock standard rows

EventAuditLog.description: Audit log entry for event membership changes. Provides complete traceability of participant additions and removals.

id.description: Unique identifier for audit log entry

eventId.description: Event ID this log entry relates to

performedBy.description: User or member who performed the action

memberId.description: Member who was added or removed

memberName.description: Name of member who was added or removed

memberRank.description: Rank of member who was added or removed

deviceInfo.description: Device information captured at time of action

locationInfo.description: Location information captured at time of action

notes.description: Optional notes or reason for action

createdAt.description: When audit log entry was created

name.description: Station name (typically same as brigade name for 1:1 relationships)

distance.description: Distance in kilometers from user location (if location provided)

relevanceScore.description: Search relevance score 0-1 (if search query provided)

BrigadeAccessToken.description: Brigade access token for kiosk mode station locking

stationId.description: Associated station ID - kiosk will be locked to this station

description.description: Optional description for the token (e.g., 'Main Kiosk', 'Mobile Tablet')

expiresAt.description: Optional expiration timestamp - token is invalid after this time

GET /api/auth/entitlements.description: Lightweight entitlement probe for the authenticated user's organisation. Returns the current entitlements object and plan code. Designed for sibling apps (e.g. AAR Studio at /aar) to check access without fetching the full /auth/me payload.

entitlements.description: null when the user has no associated organisation

updateTriggers: When adding new REST API endpoints
When modifying endpoint paths, methods, or parameters
When adding or changing Socket.io events
